questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} f\left(x^2\right)=a$ (a finite nubmer), then which of the following is/are true
questions.solution:
We have $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} f\left(x^2\right)=a \Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{x f\left(x^2\right)}{x}=a$
Applying L.H. rule we have $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} x^2 f^{\prime}\left(x^2\right)=0$
Again applying L.H. rule $=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{x^3 f^{\prime}\left(x^2\right)}{x}=0$ we get $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} x^4 f^{\prime \prime}\left(x^2\right)=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)=\frac{\cos ^{-1}(1-\{x\}) \sin ^{-1}(1-\{x\})}{\sqrt{2\{x\}}(1-\{x\})}$, then which of the following is/are correct?
[Note: $\{k\}$ denotes fractional part function of $k$.]
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\frac{\sin ^{-1}(1-\{x\}) \cos ^{-1}(1-\{x\})}{\sqrt{2\{x\}}(1-\{x\})}$
$f\left(0^{+}\right)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} \frac{\sin (1-h) \cdot \cos ^{-1}(1-h)}{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{h}(1-h)}=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\cos ^{-1}(1-h)}{\sqrt{h}}$
Let $\cos ^{-1}(1-h)=\theta \quad \Rightarrow \quad 1-h=\cos \theta \quad \Rightarrow h=1-\cos \theta$
$\therefore \quad l=\frac{\pi}{2} \frac{\theta}{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}=\frac{\pi}{2} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \frac{\theta \sqrt{1+\cos \theta}}{\sin \theta}=\frac{\pi}{2}$
III $\begin{aligned} f\left(0^{-}\right) & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0^{-}} \frac{\sin ^{-1}(1-(1-h)) \cos ^{-1}(1-(1-h))}{\sqrt{2(1-h)}(1-(1-h))} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0^{-}} \frac{\sin ^{-1} h \cdot \cos ^{-1} h}{\sqrt{2} h}=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(a^{\frac{1}{n}}+\ln b+\cos \frac{x}{\sqrt{n}}\right)^n$ where $a, b>0$ be a non-constant function and $L=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)-a}{1-\cos x}$. Identify which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The number of solution(s) of the equation $f(x)=|x|$ are 3 .
The number of solution(s) of the equation $f(x)=|x|$ are 2 .
questions.solution: $f(x)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(a^{\frac{1}{n}}+\ln b+\cos \frac{x}{\sqrt{n}}\right)^n, \quad \ln b=-1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad b=e^{-1}$ $\begin{aligned} & =e^{\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{a^{\frac{1}{n}}-1}{\frac{1}{n}}-\frac{\left(1-\cos \frac{x}{\sqrt{n}}\right)}{\frac{1}{n}}\right)}=e^{\ln a-\frac{x^2}{2}}=a e^{\frac{-x^2}{2}} \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=|x| \Rightarrow a e^{\frac{-x^2}{2}}=|x| \\ & L=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{a e^{\frac{-x^2}{2}}-a}{\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{x^2}\right) \cdot x^2}=-a \\ & \therefore \quad L+a=0 \text { and } L+a+3 b e=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $P(t)=\lim _{r \rightarrow-\infty} \sum_{r=2}^n \frac{\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}}(1-t)}{\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-1}}+1\right)\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}}+1\right)}$, then $P\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$ lies in the interval:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
P(t) & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=2}^n\left(\frac{\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}}-\sqrt{t^{2 r-1}}}{\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-1}+1}\right)\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}+1}\right)}\right)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=2}^n \frac{\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}}+1\right)-\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-1}}+1\right)}{\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-1}}+1\right)\left(\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}}+1\right)} \\
& =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=2}^n\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2 r-1}}+1}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2 r-3}}+1}\right)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2 n-1}}+1}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{t}+1}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
For $t \in(0,1)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& P(t)=1-\frac{1}{\sqrt{t}+1}=\frac{\sqrt{t}}{\sqrt{t}+1} \\
& \therefore \quad P\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)=\sqrt{2}-1 \cong 0.414 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:(0, \pi) \rightarrow R$ be a differentiable function defined as $f(x)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{1}{2^{2 r}} \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2^r}$. Then which of the following must be correct?
options: $f(x)=0$ has at least one real root
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\frac{1}{2^2} \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2^4} \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2^2}+\frac{1}{2^6} \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2^3}+\ldots \ldots \infty$
Let $g(x)=\int f(x) d x$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} \tan \frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2^2} \tan \frac{x}{2^2}+\frac{1}{2^3} \tan \frac{x}{2^3}+\ldots \ldots . \infty+C \\ & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{2^n} \cot \frac{x}{2^n}-\cot x+C \\ & =\frac{1}{x}-\cot x+C \Rightarrow f(x)=\operatorname{cosec}^2 x-\frac{1}{x^2}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime}(x)=-2 \operatorname{cosec}^2 x \cot x+\frac{2}{x^3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 2}\left(\log _3\left(a x^2+3 \mathrm{x}+1\right)\right)^{\log _{(x-1)^2} 3}=\ell$, where $\ell$ is a finite real number then
options: 'a' can have more than one values
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 2}\left(\log _3\left(a x^2+3 x+1\right)\right)$ must be equal to 1
So $4 a+7=3 \Rightarrow a=-1$
Also $l=\mathrm{e}^{\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 2}\left(\frac{\log _3\left(-\mathrm{x}^2+3 \mathrm{x}+1\right)-1}{\log _3(\mathrm{x}-1)}\right)}=\mathrm{e}^{\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 2}\left(\frac{(\mathrm{x}-1)(3-2 \mathrm{x})}{\left(-\mathrm{x}^2+3 \mathrm{x}+1\right)}\right)}=\mathrm{e}^{-1 / 3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a, b, c \in R^{+}$then $\quad \operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{k=1}^n \frac{n}{(k+a n)(k+b n)}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have $\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{k=1}^n \frac{n}{n^2\left(a+\frac{k}{n}\right)\left(b+\frac{k}{n}\right)}=\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{k=1}^n \frac{1}{\left(a+\frac{k}{n}\right)\left(b+\frac{k}{n}\right)}$
$=\int_0^1 \frac{1}{(a+x)(b+x)} d x=\frac{1}{a-b} \int_0^1 \frac{(a+x)-(b+x)}{(a+x)(b+x)} d x=\frac{1}{a-b} \int_0^1\left(\frac{1}{b+x}-\frac{1}{a+x}\right) d x$
$=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}}[\ln (\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{x})-\ln (\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{x})]_0^1=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}}\left[\ln \frac{(\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{x})}{(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{x})}\right]_0^1=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}} \ln \frac{(\mathrm{b}+1) \mathrm{a}}{(\mathrm{a}+1) \mathrm{b}} \Rightarrow(B)$ 
Now if $\mathrm{a}=\mathrm{b}$ then $\left.I=\operatorname{Lim}_{\mathrm{n} \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{\mathrm{k}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \frac{1}{\mathrm{n}} \frac{1}{\left(\mathrm{a}+\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{n}}\right)^2}=\int_0^1 \frac{d x}{(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{x})^2}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{x}}\right]_1^0=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}+1}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}(\mathrm{a}+1)} \Rightarrow D$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
